words.def: a time of great danger, difficulty or doubt when problems must be solved or important decisions must be made
words.example: an economic/a financial crisis

words.def: a standard or principle by which something is judged, or with the help of which a decision is made
words.example: The main criterion is value for money.

words.def: a person who expresses opinions about the good and bad qualities of books, music, etc.
words.example: a film/an art/a music critic

words.def: saying what you think is bad about somebody/something
words.example: You should just ignore any critical comments.

words.def: in a way that says what you think is bad about somebody/something
words.example: She spoke critically of her father.

words.def: the act of expressing disapproval of somebody/something and opinions about their faults or bad qualities; a statement showing this disapproval
words.example: to draw/face/receive criticism

words.def: to say that you think somebody/something is bad; to say what you do not like or think is wrong about somebody/something
words.example: All you ever do is criticize!

words.def: a plant that is grown in large quantities, especially as food
words.example: Sugar is an important crop on the island.

words.def: extremely important, because it will affect other things
words.example: The next few weeks are going to be crucial.

words.def: a journey by sea, visiting different places, especially as a holiday
words.example: I'd love to go on a round-the-world cruise.

words.def: a loud sound without words that expresses a strong feeling
words.example: cry of something, to give a cry of anguish/despair/pain/joy/alarm, etc.

words.def: an action or event that is a signal for somebody to do something
words.example: cue (for something), Jon's arrival was a cue for more champagne.

words.def: a medicine or medical treatment that cures an illness
words.example: cure for something, the search for a cure for cancer

words.def: having a strong desire to know about something
words.example: He is such a curious boy, always asking questions.

words.def: happening now; of the present time
words.example: The necklace would be worth over $5 000 at current prices.

words.def: the subjects that are included in a course of study or taught in a school, college, etc.
words.example: The school curriculum should be as broad as possible.

words.def: a line or surface that bends gradually; a smooth bend
words.example: curve of something, He admired the delicate curve of her ear.

words.def: having a round shape
words.example: a curved edge/surface

words.def: pretty and attractive
words.example: a cute little baby

words.def: made from milk
words.example: dairy products/produce

words.def: to be brave enough to do something
words.example: She said it as loudly as she dared.

words.def: the state of being dark, without any light
words.example: After a few minutes our eyes got used to the darkness.

words.def: facts or information, especially when examined and used to find out things or to make decisions
words.example: We collected publicly available data over a 10-day period.

words.def: an organized set of data that is stored in a computer and can be looked at and used in various ways
words.example: The database is updated monthly.

words.def: a particular day of the month, sometimes in a particular year, given in numbers and words
words.example: ‘What's the date today?’ ‘The 10th.’

words.def: a point in time by which something must be done
words.example: I prefer to work to a deadline.

words.def: causing or likely to cause death
words.example: a deadly weapon/disease

words.def: a person whose business is buying and selling a particular product
words.example: an art/antique dealer

words.def: a formal discussion of an issue at a public meeting or in a parliament. In a debate two or more speakers express opposite views and then there is often a vote on the issue.
words.example: the first ever televised presidential debate

words.def: a sum of money that somebody owes
words.example: to pay/repay a debt

words.def: a period of ten years, especially a continuous period, such as 1910–1919 or 2000–2009
words.example: the early decades of the nineteenth century

words.def: of a good enough standard or quality
words.example: a decent meal/place to live

words.def: the top outside floor of a ship or boat
words.example: on deck, I was the only person on deck at that time of night.

words.def: to say something officially or publicly
words.example: declare something, The government has declared a state of emergency.

words.def: a continuous decrease in the number, value, quality, etc. of something
words.example: decline in something, There has been a 5 per cent decline in student numbers.

words.def: to make something look more attractive by putting things on it
words.example: decorate something with something, They decorated the room with flowers and balloons.

words.def: a thing that makes something look more attractive on special occasions
words.example: Christmas/festive decorations

words.def: the process of reducing something; the amount that something is reduced by
words.example: Sales for May show a decrease compared with the same month last year.

words.def: having a large distance from the top or surface to the bottom
words.example: a deep hole/well/river

words.def: very; very much
words.example: She is deeply religious.

words.def: failure to win or to be successful
words.example: The party faces defeat in the election.

words.def: the act of protecting somebody/something from attack, criticism, etc.
words.example: in defence of somebody/something, soldiers who died in defence of their country

words.def: to protect somebody/something from attack
words.example: defend somebody/something, The role of the military is to defend the country.

words.def: a player who must stop the other team from scoring in games such as football (soccer), hockey, etc.
words.example: a passionate defender of human rights

words.def: a period of time when somebody/something has to wait because of a problem that makes something slow or late
words.example: Commuters will face long delays on the roads today.

words.def: to remove something that has been written or printed, or that has been stored on a computer
words.example: Your name has been deleted from the list.

words.def: done on purpose rather than by accident
words.example: a deliberate act of vandalism

words.def: done in a way that was planned, not by chance
words.example: She's been deliberately ignoring him all day.

words.def: a feeling of great pleasure
words.example: a feeling of sheer/pure delight

words.def: very pleased
words.example: a delighted smile

words.def: the act of taking goods, letters, etc. to the people they have been sent to
words.example: Allow 28 days for delivery.

words.def: a very strong request for something; something that somebody needs
words.example: demand for something, a demand for higher pay

words.def: a system of government in which the people of a country can vote to elect their representatives
words.example: parliamentary democracy

words.def: controlled by representatives who are elected by the people of a country; connected with this system
words.example: a democratic country

words.def: to show something clearly by giving proof or evidence
words.example: demonstrate that…, New research convincingly demonstrates that age-related memory loss is not inevitable.

words.def: a public meeting or a march (= an organized walk by many people) at which people show that they are protesting against or supporting somebody/something
words.example: to take part in/go on a demonstration

words.def: to say that something is not true
words.example: deny something, to deny an allegation/a charge/an accusation

words.def: to leave a place, especially to start a trip
words.example: depart (for…) (from…), Flights for Rome depart from Terminal 3.

words.def: needing somebody/something in order to survive or be successful
words.example: a woman with several dependent children

words.def: very sad and without hope
words.example: You mustn't let yourself get depressed.

words.def: making you feel very sad and without enthusiasm
words.example: a depressing sight/thought/experience

words.def: a medical condition in which a person feels very sad, anxious and without hope and often has physical symptoms such as being unable to sleep, etc.
words.example: She was diagnosed as having clinical depression.

words.def: the distance from the top or surface to the bottom of something; a particular distance down from the surface of something
words.example: depth of something, What's the depth of the water here?

words.def: a large area of land that has very little water and very few plants growing on it. Many deserts are covered by sand.
words.example: the Sahara Desert

words.def: to do something or show qualities that mean a particular reaction, reward or punishment is appropriate
words.example: deserve something, You deserve a rest after all that hard work.

words.def: a person whose job is to decide how things such as clothes, furniture, tools, etc. will look or work by making drawings, plans or patterns
words.example: one of the country's top fashion designers

words.def: a strong wish to have or do something
words.example: He now had enough money to satisfy all his desires.

words.def: feeling or showing that you have little hope and are ready to do anything without worrying about danger to yourself or others
words.example: The prisoners grew increasingly desperate.
